atoms.source_snippet:
origin_tx: _,
call_id: _,
index: _,
max_block: _,
initial_block: _,
frequency: _,
...
commitment_tx: None,
settlement_tx: None,
spc_calls: Default::default(),
tx_hash: None,
atoms.semantic_claim: The old RPC call helper accepts Ritual scheduled transaction request fields and initializes async/SPC fields on the constructed EVM `TxEnv` to safe defaults for eth_call/simulation paths.

destinations.evidence: Current RPC transaction request schema includes the scheduled request fields, so eth_call-style requests can deserialize them instead of dropping them at the JSON schema boundary.

Current TxEnv conversion explicitly destructures `origin_tx`, `call_id`, `index`, `max_block`, `initial_block`, and `frequency` as ignored fields, then builds a default TxEnv. Runtime async/SPC fields remain default/None for simulation, matching the old safe-default behavior.

Real recovered transaction execution paths preserve async/SPC runtime fields (`commitment_tx`, `settlement_tx`, `spc_calls`) when they come from signed transactions; eth_call request conversion is only the simulation/defaulting path.

atoms.equivalence_argument: False positive: the behavior moved into local Alloy. The request schema still carries scheduled fields, and the TxEnv conversion intentionally ignores scheduled-only request fields while leaving runtime async/SPC fields at default values for eth_call/simulation. That is the same behavior the old RPC helper encoded locally.
atoms.test_strategy: eth_call request deserialization/conversion regression with scheduled fields present
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Deserialize an eth_call TransactionRequest containing scheduled fields and convert it to TxEnv; scheduled fields should be accepted but should not install commitment/settlement/SPC runtime context.
atoms.notes: Closed as moved-to-Alloy structural equivalence; not a missing RPC FSM behavior.

summary: False positive closed: Ritual scheduled eth_call fields and safe TxEnv defaulting moved into local Alloy request/schema conversion.
notes: Scheduled request fields are present in `ritual-alloy-internal` TransactionRequest.
`ritual-alloy-evm-internal` TryIntoTxEnv explicitly ignores those request-only fields and produces default runtime async/SPC TxEnv fields for simulation.
Recovered signed transaction paths still preserve async/SPC runtime fields where execution needs them.
